rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope, preserving the AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly imported namespaces
Preserve the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scope and visibility rules when inserting annex blocks
Preserve the original AADL architecture while adding AGREE annexes
Use only valid identifiers from the current scope or explicitly imported namespaces
Follow the authoritative source for component and port information from the input AADL model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
